title: Causal TMS Study: pIC and dlPFC/PPC Gating of Ignition Threshold via Dissociable Mechanisms
description: Single-pulse TMS is applied over the right posterior insula (pIC) and dorsolateral/posterior parietal cortex (dlPFC/PPC) using fMRI-guided neuronavigation to test whether these structures causally regulate the APGI ignition threshold θₜ via dissociable mechanisms. APGI predicts that pIC TMS selectively disrupts interoceptive gating (abolishing HEP–PCI coupling, raising θₜ) while sparing exteroceptive P3b, whereas dlPFC/PPC TMS reduces PCI globally across both stimulus streams without affecting the HEP. The Perturbational Complexity Index (PCI) serves as the primary ignition proxy. Three sub-predictions are tested: (Pred 2.A) pIC and dlPFC/PPC TMS reduce PCI via dissociable mechanisms — pIC abolishes HEP–PCI coupling while dlPFC TMS does not affect HEP; (Pred 2.B) pIC TMS selectively disrupts interoceptive gating while sparing exteroceptive P3b, with Bayesian equivalence confirmed for dlPFC HEP effect; and (Pred 2.C) high-baseline IA participants show the strongest pIC TMS effects on PCI, with no equivalent accuracy × TMS-site interaction for the dlPFC site.
prediction_cluster_label: Causal pIC/dlPFC Dissociation of Interoceptive Gating vs. Global Ignition

sub_predictions.claim: pIC and frontoparietal TMS reduce PCI via dissociable mechanisms: pIC TMS abolishes HEP–PCI coupling; dlPFC/PPC TMS reduces PCI without affecting HEP.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: PCI reduced by pIC TMS (~20%) and dlPFC/PPC TMS (15–25%) vs. vertex (linear mixed-effects p < 0.05); HEP–PCI correlation abolished under pIC TMS only (site × coupling interaction p < 0.05); dlPFC TMS leaves HEP unaffected.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No PCI change vs. vertex sham across all active sites; or equivalent PCI reduction at vertex as active sites; or HEP–PCI coupling equally abolished by dlPFC TMS (no dissociation).

confirming_thresholds.dissociation_required: pIC abolishes HEP–PCI coupling; dlPFC does NOT

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: No PCI change vs. vertex sham across all active sites
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: Equivalent PCI reduction at vertex sham
falsifying_thresholds.condition_3: HEP–PCI coupling equally abolished by dlPFC TMS (no site × coupling dissociation)

sub_predictions.claim: pIC TMS selectively disrupts interoceptive gating (HEP–P3b coupling) while sparing exteroceptive P3b. dlPFC TMS reduces P3b globally without affecting HEP.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: pIC TMS: HEP–P3b coupling coefficient reduced to < 0.15 (vs. vertex baseline > 0.35); exteroceptive P3b amplitude within 10% of vertex baseline. dlPFC TMS: P3b reduced across both streams; HEP unaffected (BF₀₁ ≥ 6 for equivalence, ROPE d = [−0.15, +0.15]).
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: Uniform suppression of both interoceptive and exteroceptive P3b across pIC and dlPFC TMS sites; or HEP equally reduced by dlPFC and pIC TMS (no dissociation).

dlpfc_tms.p3b_reduction: across both interoceptive and exteroceptive streams

falsifying_thresholds.condition_1: Uniform P3b suppression across pIC and dlPFC sites (no stream specificity for pIC)
falsifying_thresholds.condition_2: HEP equally reduced by dlPFC and pIC TMS

sub_predictions.claim: High baseline IA participants show strongest pIC TMS effects on PCI, producing a significant accuracy × TMS-site interaction; effect absent for dlPFC TMS site.
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: Accuracy × TMS site interaction on PCI (p < 0.05, η² > 0.10); high-accuracy group shows larger PCI reduction under pIC TMS than low-accuracy group; interaction absent for dlPFC TMS site specifically.
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No accuracy × TMS-site interaction; high- and low-accuracy groups show equivalent PCI response across all TMS sites.

confirming_thresholds.site_specificity: interaction present for pIC site only, absent for dlPFC site

falsifying_thresholds.condition: No accuracy × TMS-site interaction across all sites

validation_notes: Prediction structurally sound. The registry (EP-2, Protocol 2) specifies pIC and dlPFC/PPC as the causal targets. The substrate specification (prefrontal/parietal cortical gating) is one plausible implementation; claustrum and mediodorsal thalamic involvement remain viable alternative substrates and must be acknowledged in the paper.

inclusion_criteria: No contraindications to TMS (Rossi et al. 2021 safety checklist)
Available structural T1-weighted MRI for neuronavigation
Resting motor threshold (RMT) determinable at FDI
IA (interoceptive accuracy) assessed pre-session for tertile grouping (Pred 2.C)
exclusion_criteria: Personal or first-degree family history of epilepsy or seizure disorder
Metallic implants in head or neck
Cochlear implants or deep brain stimulators
History of significant head injury (loss of consciousness > 5 min)
Irregular cardiac rhythm on screening ECG
participants.tms_screening: Safety checklist per Rossi et al. (2021) Clin. Neurophysiol.; written informed consent to TMS risks

apparatus.TMS_system: Magstim Rapid² with 70 mm figure-of-eight coil (active) and sham coil (angled 90°)
apparatus.neuronavigation: Brainsight 2 (Rogue Research) with individual T1-weighted MRI (1 mm isotropic, MPRAGE)

target_regions.name: Right posterior insula (pIC)
target_regions.rationale: Interoceptive precision gating; somatic marker integration. Registry EP-2 primary interoceptive site.

target_regions.name: Dorsolateral prefrontal / posterior parietal cortex (dlPFC/PPC)
target_regions.rationale: Frontoparietal ignition network; global broadcast and threshold regulation. Registry EP-2 second active site. One target per participant selected based on individual fMRI functional localiser.
target_regions.site_selection: Individual fMRI functional localiser (N-back task) used to identify highest activation cluster within dlPFC or PPC mask; site selected pre-scan, blinded to condition assignment

target_regions.name: Vertex (sham control)
target_regions.rationale: Active control for nonspecific TMS effects

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp (TMS-compatible, Brain Products GmbH) at 5000 Hz, with TMS artifact suppression
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II synchronised to EEG

trial_structure.response: Visibility rating 1–4 (Perceptual Awareness Scale, PAS), response window 2000 ms

blocks.condition_counterbalancing: Latin square across TMS-site × contrast × stream blocks; TMS site blocked to avoid coil repositioning during task

pci_protocol.artifact_suppression: Fastrak suppression + SSP projection + ICA (Hernandez-Pavon 2023 pipeline)
pci_protocol.pci_computation_software: Comolatti et al. (2019) open-source PCI toolbox

dependent_variables: PCI per TMS condition (pIC, dlPFC/PPC, vertex) — primary (Pred 2.A)
HEP amplitude (250–400 ms) per TMS condition — Pred 2.A dissociation
HEP–PCI coupling coefficient per TMS condition — Pred 2.A dissociation
HEP–P3b coupling coefficient per TMS condition — Pred 2.B
Exteroceptive P3b amplitude per TMS condition — Pred 2.B stream specificity
BF₀₁ for dlPFC TMS effect on HEP (equivalence test, ROPE d = [−0.15, +0.15]) — Pred 2.B
PAS rating distribution per TMS condition
IA (interoceptive accuracy) per participant — tertile grouping for Pred 2.C
Accuracy × TMS site interaction on PCI — Pred 2.C
Estimated θₜ shift (fitted APGI model) per TMS condition
Estimated Πⁱ_eff (fitted APGI model) per TMS condition
primary_hypothesis: Active pIC TMS reduces PCI and abolishes HEP–PCI coupling relative to vertex sham, while dlPFC/PPC TMS reduces PCI across both interoceptive and exteroceptive streams without affecting the HEP (BF₀₁ ≥ 6). High-IA participants show a larger PCI reduction under pIC TMS than low-IA participants, with this accuracy × TMS-site interaction absent for the dlPFC/PPC site.
primary_analysis: Linear mixed-effects: PCI ~ TMS_site * stream + IA_tertile + (TMS_site | participant). Site × HEP–PCI coupling interaction via LME. Bayesian equivalence for dlPFC HEP effect: BF₀₁ using JZS prior (r = 0.707), ROPE d = [−0.15, +0.15]. IA × TMS-site interaction: two-way ANOVA with η² for interaction term.

power_analysis.based_on: Rosanova et al. (2012) TMS–EEG PCI effects; typical insula TMS interoception disruption Cohen's d ≈ 0.5

safety_monitoring.adverse_event_protocol: See SOP-TMS-002; participants screened for headache, phosphenes, and discomfort after each block

caveats: Registry EP-2 specifies pIC and dlPFC/PPC as the two active TMS sites. Mediodorsal thalamus and claustrum remain viable alternative substrates for θₜ regulation but are not causal targets in this protocol. Paper captions for figures presenting TMS results must include: 'pIC and dlPFC/PPC involvement is consistent with APGI threshold predictions but does not exclude thalamic, claustrum-mediated, or GABAergic cortical inhibitory mechanisms.'
Individual fMRI-guided site selection for dlPFC vs. PPC introduces variability; pre-specified site selection criteria must be locked in the OSF pre-registration before scanning begins.
Pred 2.B Bayesian equivalence (BF₀₁ ≥ 6) for dlPFC HEP effect requires ≥ 28 participants; if n < 28, BF₀₁ analysis should still be reported with confidence interval on Cohen's d.
